Title: N6-methyladenosine RNA modification regulates light intensity-dependent terpenoid biosynthesis in Schizonepeta tenuifolia

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major volatile oil constituent contributing to medicinal value and consumer popularity of S. tenuifolia
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes geranyl diphosphate to limonene, initiating the menthone biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate branch-entry product leading to menthone; directly forms limonene
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as key enzyme and first committed step in MBP
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes limonene accumulation in light; reduced by dark-induced m6A-associated transcript destabilization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome; RT-qPCR; proteomics; m6A-IP-qPCR; VIGS of m6A writer genes; mRNA degradation assay; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves under high light and growth light; reduced in dark
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Darkness; growth light (140 μmol m−2 s−1); high light (1000 μmol m−2 s−1)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for engineering higher limonene/monoterpenoid output; can be combined with PR and m6A machinery manipulation to redirect flux
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing m6A writers altered LS differently in dark versus light, indicating context-dependent effects
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome GSA: CRA020843; proteomics iProX/ProteomeXchange: PXD051576
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In dark, LS mRNA shows strong m6A enrichment and is negatively regulated via reduced stability; in light, silencing m6A writers decreased LS transcript, suggesting opposite context-dependent regulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LS is the first committed enzyme of the menthone biosynthesis pathway and directly produces limonene from GPP. 2. LS transcript and protein levels increase with light intensity and correlate with higher limonene accumulation. 3. Dark treatment increases m6A enrichment on LS mRNA, and silencing m6A writer genes alters LS transcript stability, supporting post-transcriptional regulation of limonene biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major volatile oil constituent contributing to medicinal value and consumer popularity of S. tenuifolia
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses stereoselective reduction of pulegone to menthone
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as key terminal enzyme determining menthone accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Darkness elevates PR transcript and protein abundance and strongly increases menthone accumulation; reduced m6A deposition increases PR mRNA stability and PR protein abundance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome; RT-qPCR; proteomics; m6A-IP-qPCR; VIGS of m6A writer genes; mRNA degradation assay; cleavage-site RT-qPCR; PR m6A motif mutagenesis and Arabidopsis protoplast expression; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Dark upregulated; lower in growth light and high light
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Darkness; growth light (140 μmol m−2 s−1); high light (1000 μmol m−2 s−1)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for increasing menthone content by enhancing PR biogenesis or reducing inhibitory m6A deposition at PR transcript motifs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Regulation is condition dependent; PR mRNA showed little response to m6A-writer silencing in light
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome GSA: CRA020843; proteomics iProX/ProteomeXchange: PXD051576
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Dark samples showed lower m6A enrichment on PR than GL/HL, yet PR abundance increased; VIR silencing reduced PR m6A enrichment, slowed PR mRNA degradation, reduced local cleavage around RRACH motif, and mutation of a PR m6A motif increased PR protein accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PR catalyses the terminal reduction of pulegone to menthone and is a decisive enzyme for menthone production. 2. PR transcript and protein abundance are specifically elevated in darkness, matching strong menthone accumulation. 3. m6A writer activity negatively affects PR biogenesis by promoting PR mRNA degradation/cleavage, and weakening PR m6A deposition increases PR protein accumulation.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Post-transcriptional RNA methylation affecting mRNA stability/degradation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis of monoterpenoids via control of key MBP transcript stability
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Negatively regulates PR transcript abundance in dark; context-dependent regulation of LS transcript abundance
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts as part of m6A writer complex with HAKAI and VIR
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Silencing MTA significantly increases menthone, limonene and pulegone accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major active volatile oils contributing to medicinal value and market quality
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing MTA enhances monoterpenoid accumulation under dark and growth-light conditions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression profiling; VIGS; GC-MS metabolite profiling; RT-qPCR of pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing causes broad increases in multiple monoterpenoids and may have pleiotropic effects on transcript regulation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target to boost monoterpenoid accumulation by reducing m6A deposition
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome GSA: CRA020843; proteomics iProX/ProteomeXchange: PXD051576
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct site-specific effects on PR were less mechanistically resolved than VIR, but metabolite and transcript shifts support a regulatory role
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MTA is an m6A writer responsive to light intensity in S. tenuifolia. 2. VIGS of MTA increased menthone, limonene and pulegone levels. 3. MTA contributes to post-transcriptional control of MBP gene expression, especially PR in darkness.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: HAKAI ubiquitin ligase-like m6A writer component
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Post-transcriptional RNA methylation affecting mRNA stability/degradation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis of monoterpenoids via control of key MBP transcript stability
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Negatively regulates PR transcript abundance in dark; silencing increases LS transcript in dark but decreases LS in light
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts as part of m6A writer complex with MTA and VIR
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Silencing HAKAI significantly increases menthone, limonene and pulegone accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major active volatile oils contributing to medicinal value and market quality
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HAKAI silencing elevates monoterpenoid levels under dark and growth-light conditions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression profiling; VIGS; GC-MS metabolite profiling; RT-qPCR of pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in dark than in GL or HL
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Context-dependent effects on LS in dark versus light suggest possible unintended pathway rebalancing
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to elevate menthone and related monoterpenoids via reduced m6A-mediated repression
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome GSA: CRA020843; proteomics iProX/ProteomeXchange: PXD051576
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts broadly on multiple MBP genes including PR, LS, IPD and L3OH according to VIGS/qPCR patterns
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HAKAI is a light-responsive m6A writer component in S. tenuifolia. 2. Silencing HAKAI increases menthone, limonene and pulegone accumulation. 3. HAKAI negatively regulates PR and conditionally regulates LS transcript levels, linking RNA methylation to monoterpenoid biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Post-transcriptional RNA methylation affecting mRNA cleavage, stability and protein accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis of monoterpenoids via m6A-dependent control of PR and LS biogenesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes m6A deposition on PR transcript; negatively regulates PR mRNA stability and abundance in dark; also affects LS stability
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts as part of m6A writer complex with MTA and HAKAI
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Mechanistically validated regulator controlling menthone accumulation through PR transcript m6A status
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major active volatile oils contributing to medicinal value and market quality
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: VIR silencing increases menthone, limonene and pulegone levels and enhances PR mRNA stability/protein accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR expression profiling; VIGS; GC-MS metabolite profiling; m6A-IP-qPCR; mRNA degradation assay; cleavage-site assay; PR motif mutagenesis with protoplast protein assay
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in dark than in GL or HL
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad manipulation may affect multiple transcripts beyond PR and LS
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target to raise menthone by reducing PR m6A deposition or editing specific m6A motifs
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome GSA: CRA020843; proteomics iProX/ProteomeXchange: PXD051576
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: VIR silencing reduced m6A enrichment on PR transcripts, lowered local cleavage near RRACH motif, slowed PR mRNA decay, and supports a model where m6A controls PR biogenesis in darkness
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VIR is a core m6A writer factor that mechanistically controls monoterpenoid biosynthesis in S. tenuifolia. 2. Silencing VIR increases menthone, limonene and pulegone accumulation. 3. VIR promotes m6A deposition on PR transcript, enhancing its cleavage/degradation and thereby limiting PR protein accumulation and menthone production, especially in darkness.
